You cannot avoid the sound of humanity dying.

Palestine · the entry point
This did not
begin on
October 7.
The most documented conflict in human history. Every airstrike filmed. Every death counted. And still: legally unresolved. Still happening.
75,498+ confirmed dead · Gaza Health Ministry, Apr 2026
100,000–126,000 estimated · Max Planck Institute, Nov 2025
ICJ case pending · UN Commission confirms genocide, Sept 2025
107
YEARS.
ONE
LOGIC.
The record
1917
→ 2025
Khalidi (2020) · Pappé (2006)
UN UNISPAL · ICJ
UN Commission (Sept 2025)
1917
Balfour Declaration
Britain pledges a “national home for the Jewish people.” Palestinians not consulted — described only as “non-Jewish communities.” Jewish population at the time: ~6%.
Colonial decision
1936
Arab Revolt
General strike and armed uprising against British colonial rule. 5,000+ Palestinians killed. Revolt crushed. Immigration continues unchanged.
5,000+ killed
1947
UN Partition Plan
Resolution 181 allocates 55% of Palestine to a Jewish state — Jewish residents are 33% of the population, own ~6% of the land. No Arab state votes in favour.
Imposed partition
1948
The Nakba
30+ documented massacres. Deir Yassin: 107+ killed by forces led by future prime ministers Begin and Shamir. 700,000+ expelled. 400+ villages destroyed.
30+ massacres700K+ expelled
1967
Occupation begins
Israel occupies West Bank, Gaza, Sinai, Golan Heights. The longest military occupation in modern history. Settlement construction in direct violation of international law.
58 years · ongoing
1982
Sabra & Shatila
800–3,500 civilians massacred in Beirut refugee camps. Defence minister Sharon found to bear “personal responsibility” — later becomes Prime Minister. UN declares it an act of genocide.
Massacre
2007
Siege of Gaza
Total blockade — land, air, sea. 2.3 million enclosed in 365 km². UN: “collective punishment.” Operations: 2008–09 (1,400 killed), 2014 (2,251 killed), 2021.
18 years · ongoing
2023
Current assault
75,498+ confirmed dead (Apr 2026). 100K–126K estimated (Max Planck, 2025). Hospitals bombed. Famine declared August 2025. South Africa files ICJ case December 2023.
OngoingICJ pending
2025
UN confirms genocide
Sept 16, 2025: UN Commission of Inquiry — “Israel has committed genocide against Palestinians in the Gaza Strip.” Life expectancy fell 47% in 2024.
UN CommissionStill ongoing
Génocide
Part I — The gap
6,000,000
dead in the DRC · 0 genocide rulings · 29 years
Gaza: 75,000–126,000 dead. The world is watching. The DRC: an estimated 6 million dead since 1996. The world looked away for 29 years. Srebrenica: 8,000 dead — legally confirmed genocide. Scale does not determine recognition.
0
genocide rulings for the world’s deadliest ongoing conflict
3
legally confirmed genocides out of six cases
50yr
average delay before Armenia was recognized
Deaths vs. recognition
The numbers
do not explain
the silence.

IRC (2007) · Genocide Watch (2024) · USHMM · Max Planck (2025) · ICJ
Part II — The mechanism
classify ↓ dehumanize ↓ destroy ↓ erase
Israel. The United States. The United Kingdom. Denmark. Belgium. The method changes. The intent does not.
Soykırım
Armenia
Ottoman imperial collapse. Armenians classified as internal enemy. Deported. Marched to death. 600K–1.5M killed, 1915–17.
Confirmed genocide
Jenoside
Rwanda
Belgium engineered the Hutu/Tutsi racial divide with ID cards. Left. The categories stayed. 800,000 killed in 100 days.
Confirmed genocide
Génocide
DRC / Congo
Belgian extraction never ended. Foreign powers fight over coltan through militia proxies. An estimated 6 million dead since 1996.
No recognition
إبادة جماعية
Palestine
British Mandate, 1917. The 1948 Nakba displaced 700,000. The current assault is the continuation of a 107-year-old structure.
ICJ pending
Part III — The pivot
Denmark.
Greenland.
1960s.
No bombs. No war. No images. The most deniable method. The same intent.
The Spiralkampagnen
4,500+
women and girls fitted with IUDs without consent, 1966–1975
12
youngest age at insertion — illegal under Danish law at the time
50%
of all fertile women in Greenland had a device at one point
2025
Denmark apologises — “systematic discrimination,” not genocide
Danish Gov. Commission (2025) · JusticeInfo.net · The Guardian (2024) · NYT (2023)
Photography: Juliette Pavy, Sony World Photographer of the Year 2024
MP Aki-Matilda Høegh-Dam: “This is genocide under the UN definition.”

HOW
LONG?
Palestine. Congo. Sudan. Myanmar. And 36 more.
The logic is the same. The silence is the same.
